commands.review_summary:
Adds a Google `gemini-3.5-flash` static catalog entry and recognizes that prefix in the Gemini forward-compat resolver.

PR surface: Source +11. Total +11 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not by live execution. Current main and `v2026.6.8` lack the Google catalog/resolver row, and the model-list fallback path uses the 200,000-token default when metadata is absent.

target.title: fix(google): add gemini-3.5-flash model catalog entry
target.body:
## Summary

`gemini-3.5-flash` was missing from the bundled Google model catalog. Every sibling Gemini model ships with the correct 1,048,576-token context window, but `gemini-3.5-flash` silently fell back to the 200k `DEFAULT_CONTEXT_TOKENS` default.

## Changes

- `extensions/google/provider-catalog.ts`: Add `gemini-3.5-flash` entry with `contextWindow: 1_048_576` and `maxTokens: 65_536`
- `extensions/google/provider-models.ts`: Add forward-compat routing prefix for `gemini-3.5-flash`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: gemini-3.5-flash silently used 200k context window instead of the documented 1M input window.

Real environment tested: ubuntu-24.04, Node 24, branch fix/issue-94723-gemini-3.5-flash-catalog.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/google/provider-catalog.test.ts

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/google/provider-catalog.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8

 ✓ extensions/google/provider-catalog.test.ts (2 tests)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
   Duration  1.37s
```

Observed result after fix: The catalog test passes. The gemini-3.5-flash model now resolves in the static catalog with its correct 1,048,576 context window.

What was not tested: End-to-end with a live Google API key (requires platform-specific credentials).

Closes: #94723
